evals.prompt: Assess the condition of a 1km section of paved national road in Uganda (Kampala-Jinja Highway). Survey data: IRI from laser profiler = 5.2 m/km. Visual distress survey of 100m sample segment records: alligator cracking at medium severity covering 15% of sample area, rutting measured at 8mm average depth, and patching at low severity covering 5% of sample area. The road surface is asphalt concrete. Provide the condition rating, VCI score, and recommended intervention trigger.
evals.expected_output: IRI = 5.2 m/km classifies as Fair (paved threshold 4-6 m/km). VCI calculation: alligator cracking M/15% deduct=18, rutting L deduct=5, patching L/5% deduct=3, total deducts=26, VCI=74 (Fair). Overall condition: Fair (both IRI and VCI agree). Intervention trigger: routine maintenance with monitoring, approaching periodic trigger due to medium-severity alligator cracking.

assertions.text: Correctly classifies IRI 5.2 m/km as Fair condition for paved road using the 4-6 m/km threshold range
Calculates VCI by summing deduct values for each distress (alligator cracking, rutting, patching) and subtracting from 100
VCI score is in the range 70-80 (Fair category), reflecting the combination of medium-severity cracking and minor rutting/patching
Overall condition rating is Fair based on the worse of IRI and VCI categories (both Fair in this case)
Identifies intervention trigger as routine maintenance and notes the alligator cracking as a developing structural concern

evals.prompt:
Perform a network-level condition assessment for 5 road sections in a Ugandan district. The sections have mixed surface types and conditions:

Section A: Paved, IRI=3.5, VCI=85
Section B: Paved, IRI=7.2, VCI=45
Section C: Gravel, IRI=10, VCI=70
Section D: Gravel, IRI=18, VCI=25
Section E: Paved, IRI=4.8, VCI=62

Rank the sections by condition from worst to best, apply the correct IRI thresholds for each surface type, and identify which sections need rehabilitation, periodic maintenance, and routine maintenance.
evals.expected_output: Section D is worst (Very Poor -- gravel IRI>16, VCI<40, rehabilitation needed). Section B is next (Poor -- paved IRI 6-8 range, VCI 40-59, periodic maintenance). Section E is Fair (paved IRI 4-6, VCI 60-79, routine). Section C is Fair (gravel IRI 8-12, VCI 60-79, routine). Section A is Good (paved IRI<4, VCI>=80, routine). Different IRI thresholds correctly applied for paved vs gravel.

assertions.text: Applies different IRI thresholds for paved roads (Good <4, Fair 4-6, Poor 6-8, Very Poor >8) versus gravel roads (Good <8, Fair 8-12, Poor 12-16, Very Poor >16)
Correctly identifies Section D as worst condition (Very Poor) and Section A as best (Good)
Section B classified as Poor based on both IRI (6-8 range for paved) and VCI (40-59 range)
Identifies rehabilitation need for Section D and periodic maintenance for Section B
Uses the worse of IRI and VCI categories for overall rating on each section

evals.prompt: Assess the condition of a 3km gravel district road in Karamoja, Uganda. No IRI profiling equipment is available -- only a visual walk-through survey was conducted. The survey records the following distresses: corrugation at high severity covering 35% of sample area, potholes at medium severity covering 12% of sample area, and erosion at low severity covering 8% of sample area. No drainage condition data is available. Provide a condition rating and intervention recommendation.
evals.expected_output: Proceeds with VCI-only assessment since no IRI data. VCI calculation: corrugation H/35% deduct ~22, potholes M/12% deduct ~15, erosion L/8% deduct ~4, total deducts ~41, VCI ~59 (Poor). Flags IRI data gap. Condition: Poor. Intervention: periodic maintenance (gravel resheeting). Notes seasonal context for Karamoja and recommends drainage assessment.

assertions.text: Proceeds with VCI-only assessment when IRI data is unavailable, rather than refusing to assess
Flags the absence of IRI data as a data gap that limits assessment confidence
Calculates VCI using gravel road deduct values for corrugation, potholes, and erosion
Produces a valid condition rating (Poor or Very Poor range) and identifies periodic maintenance or rehabilitation trigger
Notes missing drainage data and recommends drainage assessment as part of maintenance planning

evals.prompt: Conduct a pavement condition assessment for a 15km section of Kenya Class C road. Available data includes visual survey results showing: crocodile cracking at severity 3 and extent 3 (30% affected), IRI 5.2 m/km, and scattered potholes at severity 3 and extent 1. Rate the overall condition using the RDM 5.1 methodology and identify the responsible road authority.
evals.expected_output: Uses RDM 5.1 PCI methodology. Deterioration indices: crocodile cracking DI=9, potholes DI=3. SCR calculation per Equation 4.3. PCI = 100 - SCR, resulting in Good to Fair range. IRI 5.2 m/km = Fair for paved road. Conservative rating uses worse indicator (Fair). Class C road is KeNHA responsibility. Recommends detailed investigation (deflection testing) given cracking severity.

assertions.text: References RDM 5.1 methodology for condition assessment, including severity and extent rating scales (1-5) and PCI calculation
Applies Kenya condition categories using PCI (Very Poor <25%, Poor 25-40%, Fair 40-60%, Good 60-80%, Very Good 80-100%)
Uses conservative rating approach -- when IRI and PCI indicators disagree, uses the worse category for overall rating
Correctly identifies KeNHA as responsible authority for Class C road in Kenya
